event: View of Mount Ararat and Saint Hripsime Church
symbols: Mount Ararat — the mountain where Noah's ark came to rest after the Flood
church at the mountain's foot — faith persisting in the shadow of biblical history
workers in the field — human labor and continuity of life in the land where salvation history began
description: A landscape painting showing the snow-capped double peak of Mount Ararat dominating the background in purple and white hues. In the middle ground, a small stone church with a pointed red-brown roof sits among green trees. In the foreground, several figures bend to work in yellow-green fields during harvest. The palette is vivid with bright greens, yellows, and the purple-blue mountain against a pale sky. The style is bold and semi-expressionist with visible brushwork.
theological_description: Mount Ararat holds a singular place in biblical geography as the resting place of Noah's ark after the Great Flood. Genesis records: 'On the seventeenth day of the seventh month the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat' (Genesis 8:4). This mountain thus marks the site where God renewed his covenant with humanity after the Flood, declaring: 'Never again will I curse the ground because of humans... As long as the earth endures, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night will never cease' (Genesis 8:21-22). The workers harvesting in the foreground are themselves a fulfillment of this promise — seedtime and harvest continuing in the very shadow of the mountain where the covenant was made. The Armenian church at Ararat's foot represents the enduring faith of one of the earliest Christian nations, which traces its spiritual heritage to the very dawn of biblical history. The majesty of the mountain — visible, permanent, unmovable — serves as a fitting monument to God's faithfulness across the millennia.

scripture_refs.reason: The ark coming to rest on the mountains of Ararat — the biblical significance of the mountain depicted
God's covenant promise that seedtime and harvest will never cease — the workers harvesting fulfill this
The rainbow covenant established at Ararat — God's promise never again to destroy by flood
